name: Изобретатель
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per Artificer level after 1st
description: Мастер магических изобретений, вплетающий заклинания в предметы. Создаёт устройства, оружие и броню с магическими свойствами.

tools: One type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice

classFeatures.name: Spellcasting
classFeatures.description: You have learned how to channel magical energy through objects. See the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Artificer spells, which appear in the Artificer spell list later in the class's description.

details.toolsRequired: You produce your Artificer spells through tools. You can use {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}, or another kind of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you have proficiency as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}, and you must have one of those focuses in hand when you cast an Artificer spell (meaning the spell has an M component when you cast it).
details.cantrips: You know two Artificer cantrips of your choice. {@spell Acid Splash|XPHB} and {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Artificer cantrip of your choice. When you reach Artificer levels 10 and 14, you learn another Artificer cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Artificer Features table.
details.spellSlots: The Artificer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Artificer spells. {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} and {@spell Grease|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Artificer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Artificer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional {@filter Artificer spells|spells|class=Artificer} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Artificer, your list of prepared spells can include six Artificer spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Artificer spells for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Artificer spells.

classFeatures.name: Tinker's Magic
classFeatures.description: You know the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while holding {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}, you can create one item in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of yourself, choosing the item from the following list.

details.duration: See the rules for the item in the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB}. The item lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, at which point it vanishes.
details.uses: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Replicate Magic Item
classFeatures.description: You have learned arcane plans that you use to make magic items.

details.plansKnown: When you gain this feature, choose four plans to learn from the Magic Item Plans (Artificer Level 2+) table (see the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG} for the items' descriptions). {@item Bag of Holding|DMG}, {@item Cap of Water Breathing|DMG}, {@item Sending Stones|DMG}, and {@item Wand of the War Mage|DMG} are recommended. Whenever you gain an Artificer level, you can replace one of the plans you know with a new plan for which you qualify. You learn another plan of your choice when you reach certain Artificer levels, as shown in the Plans Known column of the Artificer Features table. When you choose a plan to learn, you choose it from any Magic Item Plans table for which you qualify; your qualification is based on your Artificer level.
details.creatingAnItem: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create one or two different magic items if you have {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} in hand. Each item is based on one of the plans you know for this feature. If a created item requires {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, you can attune yourself to it the instant you create it. If you decide to attune to the item later, you must do so using the normal process for {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}. When you reach certain Artificer levels specified in the Magic Items column of the Artificer Features table, the number of magic items you can create at the end of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} increases. Each item you create must be based on a different plan you know. You can't have more magic items from this feature than the number shown in the Magic Items column of the Artificer Features table for your level. If you try to exceed your maximum number of magic items for this feature, the oldest item vanishes, and then the new item appears.
details.duration: A magic item created by this feature functions as the normal magic item, except its magic isn't permanent; when you die, the magic item vanishes after {@dice 1d4} days. If you replace a plan you know with a new plan, any magic item created with the replaced plan immediately vanishes.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use any Wand or Weapon created by this feature as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} in lieu of using a set of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}.
details.craftingMoreMagicItems: The Replicate Magic Item feature isn't the only way for an Artificer to make magic items. The {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG} provides rules for characters—not just Artificers—who want to make magic items of all sorts. Though many different characters can make magic items, Artificers can do it faster than others; each Artificer subclass improves a character's speed at making items of a certain category. When an Artificer crafts a magic item using the normal rules in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG}, the character gains no special connection to that item. Class features that refer to items created by your Replicate Magic Item feature don't apply to items you craft by other means.

level2.name: Common magic item that isn't a Potion, a Scroll, or cursed

level10.name: Uncommon Wondrous Item that isn't cursed

level14.name: Rare Wondrous Item that isn't cursed

classFeatures.name: Artificer Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain an Artificer subclass of your choice. The Alchemist, Armorer, Artillerist, Battle Smith, and Cartographer subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Artificer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are unlocked by the Artificer levels specified in the subclass.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Artificer levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Subclass feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Artificer Subclass.

classFeatures.name: Magic Item Tinker
classFeatures.description: Your {@variantrule Replicate Magic Item} feature gains the following options.

details.chargeMagicItem: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can touch a magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and that uses charges. You expend a level 1+ spell slot and recharge the item. The number of charges the item regains is equal to the level of spell slot expended.
details.drainMagicItem: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can touch a magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and cause the item to vanish, converting its magical energy into a spell slot. The slot is level 1 if the item is Common or level 2 if the item is Uncommon or Rare. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Any spell you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.transmuteMagicItem: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch one magic item within 5 feet of yourself that you created with Replicate Magic Item and transform it into a different magic item. The resulting item must be based on a magic item plan you know. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Flash of Genius
classFeatures.description: When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails an ability check or a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add a bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1). You can take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Subclass feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Artificer Subclass.

classFeatures.name: Magic Item Adept
classFeatures.description: You can now attune to up to four magic items at once.

classFeatures.name: Spell-Storing Item
classFeatures.description: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can touch one Simple or Martial weapon or one item that you can use as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}, and you store a spell in it, choosing a level 1, 2, or 3 Artificer spell that has a casting time of an action and doesn't require a Material component that is consumed by the spell (you needn't have the spell prepared). While holding the object, a creature can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to produce the spell's effect from it, using your spellcasting ability modifier. If the spell requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, the creature must concentrate. Once a creature has used the object to produce the spell's effect, the object can't be used this way again until the start of the creature's next turn. The spell stays in the object until it's been used a number of times equal to twice your Intelligence modifier (minimum of twice) or until you use this feature again to store a spell in an object.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Advanced Artifice
classFeatures.description: You gain the following benefits.

details.magicItemSavant: You can now attune to up to five magic items at once.
details.refreshedGenius: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain one expended use of your Flash of Genius feature.

classFeatures.name: Subclass feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Artificer Subclass.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Magic Item Master
classFeatures.description: You can now attune to up to six magic items at once.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Energy Resistance|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Soul of Artifice
classFeatures.description: You have developed a mystical connection to your magic items, which you can draw on for aid. You gain the following benefits.

details.cheatDeath: If you're reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, you can disintegrate any number of Uncommon or Rare magic items created by your Replicate Magic Item feature. If you do so, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead change to a number equal to 20 times the number of magic items disintegrated.
details.magicalGuidance: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain all expended uses of your Flash of Genius if you have {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to at least one magic item.

name: Варвар
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d12 (or 7) + your Constitution modifier per Barbarian level after 1st
description: Варвар — свирепый воин, черпающий силу из первобытной ярости. Непревзойдённый боец ближнего боя.

classFeatures.name: Rage
classFeatures.description: You can imbue yourself with a primal power called Rage, a force that grants you extraordinary might and resilience. You can enter it as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} if you aren't wearing Heavy armor. You can enter your Rage the number of times shown for your Barbarian level in the Rages column of the Barbarian Features table. You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

details.damageResistance: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
details.rageDamage: When you make an attack using Strength—with either a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}—and deal damage to the target, you gain a bonus to the damage that increases as you gain levels as a Barbarian, as shown in the Rage Damage column of the Barbarian Features table.
details.strengthAdvantage: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
details.noConcentrationOrSpells: You can't maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and you can't cast spells.
details.duration: The Rage lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you don Heavy armor or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. If your Rage is still active on your next turn, you can extend the Rage for another round by doing one of the following: Make an attack roll against an enemy. Force an enemy to make a saving throw. Take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to extend your Rage. Each time the Rage is extended, it lasts until the end of your next turn. You can maintain a Rage for up to 10 minutes.

classFeatures.name: Unarmored Defense
classFeatures.description: While you aren't wearing any armor, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Constitution modifiers. You can use a {@item Shield|XPHB} and still gain this benefit.

classFeatures.name: Weapon Mastery
classFeatures.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice, such as {@item Greataxes|XPHB} and {@item Handaxes|XPHB}. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Barbarian levels, you gain the ability to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Barbarian Features table.

classFeatures.name: Danger Sense
classFeatures.description: You gain an uncanny sense of when things aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge perils. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

classFeatures.name: Reckless Attack
classFeatures.description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with increased ferocity. When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} during that time.

classFeatures.name: Barbarian Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Barbarian subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Barbarian levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Barbarian level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Primal Knowledge
classFeatures.description: You gain proficiency in another skill of your choice from the skill list available to Barbarians at level 1. In addition, while your Rage is active, you can channel primal power when you attempt certain tasks; whenever you make an ability check using one of the following skills, you can make it as a Strength check even if it normally uses a different ability: {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Perception}, {@skill Stealth}, or {@skill Survival}. When you use this ability, your Strength represents primal power coursing through you, honing your agility, bearing, and senses.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Barbarian levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Extra Attack
classFeatures.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeatures.name: Fast Movement
classFeatures.description: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

classFeatures.name: Feral Instinct
classFeatures.description: Your instincts are so honed that you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.

classFeatures.name: Instinctive Pounce
classFeatures.description: As part of the {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} you take to enter your Rage, you can move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Brutal Strike
classFeatures.description: If you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo any {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on one Strength-based attack roll of your choice on your turn. The chosen attack roll mustn't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. If the chosen attack roll hits, the target takes an extra {@dice 1d10} damage of the same type dealt by the weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, and you can cause one Brutal Strike effect of your choice. You have the following effect options.

details.forcefulBlow: {@b Forceful Blow.} The target is pushed 15 feet straight away from you. You can then move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} straight toward the target without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.
details.hamstringBlow: {@b Hamstring Blow.} The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn. A target can be affected by only one Hamstring Blow at a time—the most recent one.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

classFeatures.name: Relentless Rage
classFeatures.description: Your Rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while your Rage is active and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead change to a number equal to twice your Barbarian level. Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the DC resets to 10.

classFeatures.name: Improved Brutal Strike
classFeatures.description: You have honed new ways to attack furiously. The following effects are now among your Brutal Strike options.

details.staggeringBlow: {@b Staggering Blow.} The target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes, and it can't make {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.
details.sunderingBlow: {@b Sundering Blow.} Before the start of your next turn, the next attack roll made by another creature against the target gains a +5 bonus to the roll. An attack roll can gain only one Sundering Blow bonus.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Barbarian subclass.

classFeatures.name: Persistent Rage
classFeatures.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can regain all expended uses of Rage. After you regain uses of Rage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, your Rage is so fierce that it now lasts for 10 minutes without needing to do anything to extend it from round to round. Your Rage ends early if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition (not just the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition) or don Heavy armor.

classFeatures.name: Improved Brutal Strike
classFeatures.description: The extra damage of your Brutal Strike increases to {@dice 2d10}. In addition, you can use two different Brutal Strike effects whenever you use your Brutal Strike feature.

classFeatures.name: Indomitable Might
classFeatures.description: If your total for a Strength check or Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Irresistible Offense|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Primal Champion
classFeatures.description: You embody the power of the wilds. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

name: Бард
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per Bard level after 1st
description: Бард — вдохновляющий маг, чья сила проистекает из музыки, слов и танца. Мастер на все руки.

classFeatures.name: Bardic Inspiration
classFeatures.description: You can supernaturally inspire others through words, music, or dance. This inspiration is represented by your Bardic Inspiration die, which is a {@dice d6}.

details.usingBardicInspiration: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can inspire another creature within 60 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains one of your Bardic Inspiration dice. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time. Once within the next hour when the creature fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, the creature can roll the Bardic Inspiration die and add the number rolled to the {@dice d20}, potentially turning the failure into a success. A Bardic Inspiration die is expended when it's rolled.
details.numberOfUses: You can confer a Bardic Inspiration die a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.atHigherLevels: Your Bardic Inspiration die changes when you reach certain Bard levels, as shown in the Bardic Die column of the Bard Features table. The die becomes a {@dice d8} at level 5, a {@dice d10} at level 10, and a {@dice d12} at level 15.

classFeatures.name: Spellcasting
classFeatures.description: You have learned to cast spells through your bardic arts. See chapter 7 for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Bard spells, which appear in the {@filter Bard spell list}.

details.cantrips: You know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Bard spell list}. {@spell Dancing Lights|XPHB} and {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Bard spell list}. When you reach Bard levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Bard spell list}, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Bard Features table.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose four level 1 spells from the {@filter Bard spell list}. {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, {@spell Color Spray|XPHB}, {@spell Dissonant Whispers|XPHB}, and {@spell Healing Word|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Bard levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Bard Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Bard spell list} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. If another Bard feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Bard spells for you.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Bard spell for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Bard spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use a {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Bard spells.

classFeatures.name: Expertise
classFeatures.description: You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. {@skill Performance} and {@skill Persuasion} are recommended if you have proficiency in them. At Bard level 9, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

classFeatures.name: Jack of All Trades
classFeatures.description: You can add half your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (round down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack and that doesn't otherwise use your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. For example, if you make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check and lack {@skill Athletics} proficiency, you can add half your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} to the check.

classFeatures.name: Bard Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Bard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Bard levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Bard level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Bard levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Font of Inspiration
classFeatures.description: You now regain all your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, you can expend a spell slot (no action required) to regain one expended use of Bardic Inspiration.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Bard subclass.

classFeatures.name: Countercharm
classFeatures.description: You can use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause the save to be rerolled, and the new roll has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Expertise
classFeatures.description: You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

classFeatures.name: Magical Secrets
classFeatures.description: You have collected magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Whenever you prepare your Bard spells, two of the spells you prepare can be from any spell list. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard Features table, or a cantrip. The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Bard subclass.

classFeatures.name: Superior Inspiration
classFeatures.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain two expended uses of it.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Spell Recall|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Words of Creation
classFeatures.description: You have mastered two of the Words of Creation: the words of life and death. You therefore always have {@spell Power Word Heal|XPHB} and {@spell Power Word Kill|XPHB} prepared. When you use either spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within 10 feet of the first target.

name: Жрец
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per Cleric level after 1st
description: Божественный заклинатель, служащий высшей силе. Целитель и защитник веры.

classFeatures.name: Spellcasting
classFeatures.description: You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Cleric spells, which appear on the {@filter Cleric spell list} later in the class's description.

details.cantrips: You know three cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Cleric spell list}. {@spell Guidance|XPHB}, {@spell Sacred Flame|XPHB}, and {@spell Thaumaturgy|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Cleric level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Cleric spell list}. When you reach Cleric levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Cleric spell list}, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Cleric Features table.
details.spellSlots: The Cleric Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose four level 1 spells from the {@filter Cleric spell list}. {@spell Bless|XPHB}, {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, {@spell Guiding Bolt|XPHB}, and {@spell Shield of Faith|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Cleric levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Cleric Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Cleric spell list} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. If another Cleric feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Cleric spells for you.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Cleric spells for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Cleric spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use a {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Cleric spells.

classFeatures.name: Divine Order
classFeatures.description: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice.

details.protector: {@b Protector.} Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with {@filter Martial weapons|XPHB} and training with {@item Heavy armor|XPHB}.
details.thaumaturge: {@b Thaumaturge.} You know one extra cantrip from the {@filter Cleric spell list}. In addition, your mystical connection to the divine gives you a bonus to your Intelligence ({@skill Arcana} or {@skill Religion}) checks. The bonus equals your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

classFeatures.name: Channel Divinity
classFeatures.description: You can channel divine energy directly from the Outer Planes to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Divine Spark and Turn Undead, each of which is described below. Each time you use this class's Channel Divinity, choose which Channel Divinity effect from this class to create. You gain additional effect options at higher Cleric levels. You can use this class's Channel Divinity twice. You regain one of its expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You gain additional uses when you reach certain Cleric levels, as shown in the Channel Divinity column of the Cleric Features table. If a Channel Divinity effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals the spell save DC from this class's Spellcasting feature.

details.divineSpark: {@b Divine Spark.} As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you point your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} at another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself and focus divine energy at it. Roll {@dice 1d8} and add your Wisdom modifier. You either restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to the creature equal to that total or force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice) equal to that total. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage (round down). You roll an additional {@dice d8} when you reach Cleric levels 7 ({@dice 2d8}), 13 ({@dice 3d8}), and 18 ({@dice 4d8}).
details.turnUndead: {@b Turn Undead.} As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} and censure Undead creatures. Each Undead of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its save, it has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} and {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} conditions for 1 minute. For that duration, it tries to move as far from you as it can on its turns. This effect ends early on the creature if it takes any damage, if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, or if you die.

classFeatures.name: Cleric Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Cleric subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Cleric levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Cleric level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Cleric levels 8, 12, 16, and 18.

classFeatures.name: Sear Undead
classFeatures.description: Whenever you use Turn Undead, you can roll a number of {@dice d8}s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of {@dice 1d8}) and add the rolls together. Each Undead that fails its saving throw against that use of Turn Undead takes Radiant damage equal to the roll's total. This damage doesn't end the turn effect.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Cleric subclass.

classFeatures.name: Blessed Strikes
classFeatures.description: Divine power infuses you in battle. You gain one of the following options of your choice (if you get either option from a Cleric subclass feature, you aren't limited to the options below).

details.divineStrike: {@b Divine Strike.} Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 1d8} Necrotic or Radiant damage (your choice).
details.potentSpellcasting: {@b Potent Spellcasting.} You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Cleric cantrip.

classFeatures.name: Divine Intervention
classFeatures.description: You can call on your deity or pantheon to intervene on your behalf. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose any Cleric spell of level 5 or lower that doesn't require a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast. As part of the same action, you cast that spell without expending a spell slot or needing Material components. You can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Improved Blessed Strikes
classFeatures.description: The option you chose for Blessed Strikes grows more powerful.

details.divineStrike: {@b Divine Strike.} The extra damage of your Divine Strike increases to {@damage 2d8}.
details.potentSpellcasting: {@b Potent Spellcasting.} When you cast a Cleric cantrip and deal damage to a creature with it, you can give vitality to yourself or another creature within 60 feet of yourself, granting a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Wisdom modifier.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Cleric subclass.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Fate|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Greater Divine Intervention
classFeatures.description: You can call on even more powerful divine intervention. When you use your Divine Intervention feature, you can choose {@spell Wish|XPHB} when you select a spell. If you do so, you can't use Divine Intervention again until you finish {@dice 2d4} {@variantrule Long Rests|XPHB}.

name: Друид
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per Druid level after 1st
description: Хранитель природы, черпающий магию из первозданных сил мира. Может принимать облик зверей.

classFeatures.name: Druidic
classFeatures.description: You know Druidic, the secret language of Druids. While learning this ancient tongue, you also unlocked the magic of communicating with animals; you always have the {@spell Speak with Animals} spell prepared. You can use Druidic to leave hidden messages. You and others who know Druidic automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message's presence with a successful DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check but can't decipher it without magic.

classFeatures.name: Primal Order
classFeatures.description: You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice.

details.magician: {@b Magician.} You know one extra cantrip from the {@filter Druid spell list}. In addition, your mystical connection to nature gives you a bonus to your Intelligence ({@skill Arcana} or {@skill Nature}) checks. The bonus equals your Wisdom modifier (minimum bonus of +1).
details.warden: {@b Warden.} Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with {@filter Martial weapons} and training with {@item Medium armor|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Spellcasting
classFeatures.description: You have learned to cast spells through studying the mystical forces of nature. See {@variantrule chapter 7|XPHB} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Druid spells, which appear on the {@filter Druid spell list} later in the class's description.

details.cantrips: You know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Druid spell list}. {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} and {@spell Produce Flame|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Druid level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Druid spell list}. When you reach Druid levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Druid spell list}, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Druid Features table.
details.spellSlots: The Druid Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.preparedSpells: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose four level 1 spells from the {@filter Druid spell list}. {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB}, {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB}, and {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} are recommended. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Druid levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Druid Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the {@filter Druid spell list} until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 3 Druid, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination. If another Druid feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Druid spells for you.
details.changingPreparedSpells: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells with other Druid spells for which you have spell slots.
details.spellcastingAbility: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Druid spells.
details.spellcastingFocus: You can use a {@item Druidic Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Druid spells.

classFeatures.name: Wild Companion
classFeatures.description: You can summon a nature spirit that assumes an animal form to aid you. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend a spell slot or a use of Wild Shape to cast the {@spell Find Familiar} spell without Material components. When you cast the spell in this way, the familiar is Fey and disappears when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Wild Shape
classFeatures.description: The power of nature allows you to assume the form of an animal. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you shape-shift into a Beast form that you have learned for this feature (see "Known Forms" below). You stay in that form for a number of hours equal to half your Druid level or until you use Wild Shape again, have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition, or die. You can also leave the form early as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.

details.numberOfUses: You can use Wild Shape twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You gain additional uses when you reach certain Druid levels, as shown in the Wild Shape column of the Druid Features table.
details.knownForms: You know four Beast forms for this feature, chosen from among Beast stat blocks that have a maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of 1/4 and that lack a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} (see {@variantrule appendix B|XPHB} for stat block options). The {@creature Rat}, {@creature Riding Horse}, {@creature Spider}, and {@creature Wolf} are recommended. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one of your known forms with another eligible form. When you reach certain Druid levels, your number of known forms and the maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} for those forms increases, as shown in the Beast Shapes table. In addition, starting at level 8, you can adopt a form that has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}. When choosing known forms, you may look in the {@item Monster Manual} or elsewhere for eligible Beasts if the Dungeon Master permits you to do so.
details.rulesWhileShapeShifted: While in a form, you retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak, and the following rules apply:
details.temporaryHitPoints: When you assume a Wild Shape form, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Druid level.
details.gameStatistics: Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast's stat block, but you retain your creature type; {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}; {@item Hit Point Dice|XPHB}; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats. You also retain your skill and saving throw proficiencies and use your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} for them, in addition to gaining the proficiencies of the creature. If a skill or saving throw modifier in the Beast's stat block is higher than yours, use the one in the stat block.
details.noSpellcasting: You can't cast spells, but shape-shifting doesn't break your {@status Concentration} or otherwise interfere with a spell you've already cast.
details.objects: Your ability to handle objects is determined by the form's limbs rather than your own. In addition, you choose whether your equipment falls in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it's practical for the new form to wear a piece of equipment based on the creature's size and shape. Your equipment doesn't change size or shape to match the new form, and any equipment that the new form can't wear must either fall to the ground or merge with the form. Equipment that merges with the form has no effect while you're in that form.

classFeatures.name: Druid Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Druid subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Druid levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Druid level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Druid levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Wild Resurgence
classFeatures.description: Once on each of your turns, if you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot (no action required). In addition, you can expend one use of Wild Shape (no action required) to give yourself a level 1 spell slot, but you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Druid subclass.

classFeatures.name: Elemental Fury
classFeatures.description: The might of the elements flows through you. You gain one of the following options of your choice.

details.potentSpellcasting: {@b Potent Spellcasting.} You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip.
details.primalStrike: {@b Primal Strike.} Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form's attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 1d8} Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Druid subclass.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Druid subclass.

classFeatures.name: Improved Elemental Fury
classFeatures.description: The option you chose for Elemental Fury grows more powerful, as detailed below.

details.potentSpellcasting: {@b Potent Spellcasting.} When you cast a Druid cantrip with a range of 10 feet or greater, the spell's range increases by 300 feet.
details.primalStrike: {@b Primal Strike.} The extra damage of your Primal Strike increases to {@damage 2d8}.

classFeatures.name: Beast Spells
classFeatures.description: While using Wild Shape, you can cast spells in Beast form, except for any spell that has a Material component with a cost specified or that consumes its Material component.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Dimensional Travel|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Archdruid
classFeatures.description: The vitality of nature constantly blooms within you, granting you the following benefits.

details.evergreenWildShape: {@b Evergreen Wild Shape.} Whenever you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of Wild Shape left, you regain one expended use of it.
details.natureMagician: {@b Nature Magician.} You can convert uses of Wild Shape into a spell slot (no action required). Choose a number of your unexpended uses of Wild Shape and convert them into a single spell slot, with each use contributing 2 spell levels. For example, if you convert two uses of Wild Shape, you produce a level 4 spell slot. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
details.longevity: {@b Longevity.} The primal magic that you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every ten years that pass, your body ages only one year.

name: Воин
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d10 (or 6) + your Constitution modifier per Fighter level after 1st
description: Мастер боевых искусств, владеющий любым оружием и доспехами. Непревзойдённый тактик.

classFeatures.name: Fighting Style
classFeatures.description: You have honed your martial prowess and gain a {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice. {@feat Defense|XPHB} is recommended. Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat.

classFeatures.name: Second Wind
classFeatures.description: You have a limited well of physical and mental stamina that you can draw on. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can use it to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Fighter level. You can use this feature twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you reach certain Fighter levels, you gain more uses of this feature, as shown in the Second Wind column of the Fighter Features table.

classFeatures.name: Weapon Mastery
classFeatures.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of three kinds of {@filter Simple|XPHB} or {@filter Martial|XPHB} weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of your weapon choices. When you reach certain Fighter levels, you gain the ability to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Fighter Features table.

classFeatures.name: Action Surge
classFeatures.description: You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action, except the {@action Magic|XPHB} action. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Starting at level 17, you can use it twice before a rest but only once on a turn.

classFeatures.name: Tactical Mind
classFeatures.description: You have a mind for tactics on and off the battlefield. When you fail an ability check, you can expend a use of your Second Wind to push yourself toward success. Rather than regaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you roll {@dice 1d10} and add the number rolled to the ability check, potentially turning it into a success. If the check still fails, this use of Second Wind isn't expended.

classFeatures.name: Fighter Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Fighter subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Fighter levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Fighter level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Fighter levels 6, 8, 12, 14, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Extra Attack
classFeatures.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeatures.name: Tactical Shift
classFeatures.description: Whenever you activate your Second Wind with a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

classFeatures.name: Indomitable
classFeatures.description: If you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Fighter level. You must use the new roll, and you can't use this feature again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can use this feature twice before a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} starting at level 13 and three times before a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} starting at level 17.

classFeatures.name: Tactical Master
classFeatures.description: When you attack with a weapon whose {@variantrule mastery property|XPHB} you can use, you can replace that property with the {@variantrule Push|XPHB}, {@variantrule Sap|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Slow|XPHB} property for that attack.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

classFeatures.name: Two Extra Attacks
classFeatures.description: You can attack three times instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeatures.name: Studied Attacks
classFeatures.description: You study your opponents and learn from each attack you make. If you make an attack roll against a creature and miss, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Fighter subclass.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Combat Prowess|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Three Extra Attacks
classFeatures.description: You can attack four times instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

name: Стрелок
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per Gunslinger level after 1st
description: Стрелок — мастер огнестрельного оружия, сочетающий молниеносную реакцию, точную стрельбу и дерзкие манёвры. Рискуя жизнью ради победы, стрелки полагаются на ловкость, харизму и специальные кости риска.

classFeatures.name: Fighting Style
classFeatures.description: You gain a {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice. If you choose a feat, such as {@feat Great Weapon Fighting|XPHB}, that requires you to hold a Melee weapon in one or two hands, you can use that feat with Ranged weapons. Whenever you gain a Gunslinger level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat.

classFeatures.name: Quick Draw
classFeatures.description: You're adept at drawing and firing before others have time to react, granting you the following benefits.

details.initiative: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls.
details.doubleDraw: You can draw or stow two weapons that lack {@property Two-Handed|XPHB} when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.

classFeatures.name: Weapon Mastery
classFeatures.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of two kinds of {@filter Simple or Martial Ranged weapons|items|type=martial ranged weapon} of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Gunslinger levels, you gain the ability to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Gunslinger Features table.

classFeatures.name: Critical Shot
classFeatures.description: Your attack rolls with Ranged weapons can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}. At Gunslinger level 9, your attack rolls with Ranged weapons score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 18–20. At Gunslinger level 17, they score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 17–20.

classFeatures.name: Risk
classFeatures.description: You can perform incredible feats of daring fueled by special dice called Risk Dice.

details.riskDice: You have four Risk Dice, which are d8s. A Risk Die is expended when you use it. You regain all expended Risk Dice when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Your Risk Die changes and more Risk Dice become available as shown on the Risk Dice column of the Gunslinger Features table.
details.maneuvers: You can expend Risk Dice to perform maneuvers. Your maneuver options are detailed later in the class description.
details.savingThrows: If a maneuver requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Maneuver Options
classFeatures.description: The following maneuver options are available to all Gunslingers.

details.biteTheBullet: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one Risk Die to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled on the die plus your Gunslinger level.
details.blindfire: You can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend one Risk Die to gain {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet until the end of the current turn.
details.dodgeRoll: You can expend one Risk Die as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to move up to 15 feet and reload any Ranged weapon you are holding. This movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} and is unaffected by {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
details.grazingShot: When you miss with a ranged attack roll using a weapon, you can expend one Risk Die (no action required) to deal damage to that creature equal to a roll of the die plus your Dexterity modifier (minimum of 1). This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.
details.maverickSpirit: When you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma ability check or saving throw, you can expend one Risk Die to add it to the roll, potentially turning it into a success. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.
details.skinOfYourTeeth: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} and expend one Risk Die to dodge out of harm's way. Roll the die and add the number rolled to your AC against this attack, potentially causing it to miss.

classFeatures.name: Gunslinger Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Gunslinger subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Gunslinger levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Gunslinger level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Gunslinger levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Extra Attack
classFeatures.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeatures.name: Gut Shot
classFeatures.description: Whenever you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against a Large or smaller creature with a ranged weapon using a weapon, the projectile lodges itself in the target. For 1 minute or until the target replaces one of its attacks with dislodging the projectile, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Gunslinger Subclass.

classFeatures.name: Evasion
classFeatures.description: When you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. You don't benefit from this feature if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Critical Shot
classFeatures.description: Your attack rolls with Ranged weapons can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 18, 19, or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Gunslinger Subclass.

classFeatures.name: Overkill
classFeatures.description: When you deal damage with a Ranged weapon that doesn't add your ability modifier to the roll, you add your ability modifier nonetheless. If you already add your modifier to the damage roll, the target takes an extra {@dice 1d6} damage of the weapon's type. Note that weapons that have the Firearm property don't add your ability modifier to damage rolls.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Cheat Death
classFeatures.description: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and not killed outright, you can drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} instead, and you regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Gunslinger level. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Gunslinger Subclass.

classFeatures.name: Dire Gambit
classFeatures.description: Whenever you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} or score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you regain one expended Risk Die.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeatures.name: Critical Shot
classFeatures.description: Your attack rolls with Ranged weapons can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 17, 18, 19, or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

classFeatures.name: Deft Maneuver
classFeatures.description: You gain a special additional {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} that you can take once on each of your turns. You can take this special {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} only to use a maneuver.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an {@feat Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Irresistible Offense|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Headshot
classFeatures.description: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against a creature using a Ranged weapon, you can choose for it to be a Headshot. If the creature has less than 100 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it dies. Otherwise, it takes an extra {@dice 10d10} damage of the weapon's type. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending three Risk Dice (no action required).

name: Монах
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per Monk level after 1st
description: Мастер боевых искусств, использующий внутреннюю энергию ки для невероятных подвигов.

weapons: Martial weapons that have the Light property

optionA: Artisan's Tools or Musical Instrument chosen for the tool proficiency above

classFeatures.name: Martial Arts
classFeatures.description: Your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and Monk weapons, which are the following: Simple Melee Weapons and Martial Melee Weapons that have the {@property Light} property. You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only Monk weapons and you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}.

details.bonusUnarmedStrike: {@b Bonus Unarmed Strike.} You can make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
details.martialArtsDie: {@b Martial Arts Die.} You can roll {@dice 1d6} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} or Monk weapons. This changes as you gain Monk levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Monk Features table.
details.dexterousAttacks: {@b Dexterous Attacks.} You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier for the attack and damage rolls of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} and Monk weapons. In addition, when you use the Grapple or Shove option of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier to determine the save DC.

classFeatures.name: Unarmored Defense
classFeatures.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Wisdom modifiers.

classFeatures.name: Monk's Focus
classFeatures.description: Your focus and physical training allow you to harness a well of extraordinary energy within yourself. This energy is represented by Focus Points. Your Monk level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Focus Points column of the Monk Features table. You can expend these points to enhance or fuel certain Monk features. You start knowing three such features: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind, each of which is detailed below. When you expend a Focus Point, it is unavailable until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, at the end of which you regain all your expended points. Some features that use Focus Points require your target to make a saving throw. The save DC equals 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}.

details.flurryOfBlows: {@b Flurry of Blows.} You can expend 1 Focus Point to make two {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
details.patientDefense: {@b Patient Defense.} You can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the {@action Disengage|XPHB} and the {@action Dodge|XPHB} actions as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}.
details.stepOfTheWind: {@b Step of the Wind.} You can take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}. Alternatively, you can expend 1 Focus Point to take both the {@action Disengage|XPHB} and {@action Dash|XPHB} actions as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, and your jump distance is doubled for the turn.

classFeatures.name: Unarmored Movement
classFeatures.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}. This bonus increases when you reach certain Monk levels, as shown on the Monk Features table.

classFeatures.name: Uncanny Metabolism
classFeatures.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can regain all expended Focus Points. When you do so, roll your Martial Arts die, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Monk level plus the number rolled. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Deflect Attacks
classFeatures.description: When an attack roll hits you and its damage includes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the attack's total damage against you. The reduction equals {@dice 1d10} plus your Dexterity modifier and Monk level. If you reduce the damage to 0, you can expend 1 Focus Point to redirect some of the attack's force. If you do so, choose a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself if the attack was a melee attack or a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself that isn't behind {@variantrule Total Cover|XPHB} if the attack was a ranged attack. That creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Dexterity modifier. The damage is the same type dealt by the attack.

classFeatures.name: Monk Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Monk subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Monk levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Monk level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monk levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Slow Fall
classFeatures.description: You can take a {@action Reaction|XPHB} when you fall to reduce any damage you take from the fall by an amount equal to five times your Monk level.

classFeatures.name: Extra Attack
classFeatures.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

classFeatures.name: Stunning Strike
classFeatures.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a Monk weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Focus Point to attempt a stunning strike. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the start of your next turn, and the next attack roll made against the target before then has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Empowered Strikes
classFeatures.description: Whenever you deal damage with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it can deal your choice of Force damage or its normal damage type.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

classFeatures.name: Evasion
classFeatures.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail. You don't benefit from this feature if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

classFeatures.name: Acrobatic Movement
classFeatures.description: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the movement.

classFeatures.name: Heightened Focus
classFeatures.description: Your Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind gain the following benefits.

details.flurryOfBlows: {@b Flurry of Blows.} You can expend 1 Focus Point to use Flurry of Blows and make three {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} with it instead of two.
details.patientDefense: {@b Patient Defense.} When you expend a Focus Point to use Patient Defense, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die.
details.stepOfTheWind: {@b Step of the Wind.} When you expend a Focus Point to use Step of the Wind, you can choose a willing creature within 5 feet of yourself that is Large or smaller. You move the creature with you until the end of your turn. The creature's movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Self-Restoration
classFeatures.description: Through sheer force of will, you can remove one of the following conditions from yourself at the end of each of your turns: {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}. In addition, forgoing food and drink doesn't give you levels of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

classFeatures.name: Deflect Energy
classFeatures.description: You can now use your Deflect Attacks feature against attacks that deal any damage type, not just Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing.

classFeatures.name: Disciplined Survivor
classFeatures.description: Your physical and mental discipline grant you proficiency in all saving throws. Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can expend 1 Focus Point to reroll it, and you must use the new roll.

classFeatures.name: Perfect Focus
classFeatures.description: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and don't use Uncanny Metabolism, you regain expended Focus Points until you have 4 if you have 3 or fewer.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
classFeatures.description: You gain a feature from your Monk subclass.

classFeatures.name: Superior Defense
classFeatures.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 3 Focus Points to perfectly bolster yourself against harm for 1 minute or until you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. During that time, you have Resistance to all damage except Force damage.

classFeatures.name: Epic Boon
classFeatures.description: You gain an {@variantrule Epic Boon|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {@feat Boon of Irresistible Offense|XPHB} is recommended.

classFeatures.name: Body and Mind
classFeatures.description: You have developed your body and mind to new heights. Your Dexterity and Wisdom scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

name: Охотник на монстров
hitPointsAtHigherLevels: d10 (or 6) + your Constitution modifier per Monster Hunter level after 1st
description: Закалённый охотник, посвятивший жизнь уничтожению сверхъестественных угроз. Использует знания о монстрах и древние техники для выслеживания и уничтожения порождений тьмы.

multiclassing.prerequisite: Strength 13 and Intelligence 13 or Dexterity 13 and Intelligence 13

classFeatures.name: Monster Grimoire
classFeatures.description: You create or inherit a repository of knowledge known as a Monster Grimoire. Each Monster Hunter carries a grimoire, a manual on dangerous creatures. A Monster Hunter's grimoire is an object entirely unique to each hunter. It may be a leather tome recovered from a dusty library with notes made in the margins, a set of blood-stained scrolls inherited from a previous hunter, or a precious stone with a communing spirit held within.

details.lostGrimoire: If a Monster Hunter ever loses a grimoire, the hunter has retained enough knowledge to not suffer immediate drawbacks. However, the hunter can't add to the grimoire until it is restored and therefore can't take another Monster Hunter level until a lost grimoire has been replaced. A grimoire can be recreated by spending 8 hours and 50 GP worth of materials such as books, scrolls, and ink.
details.creatureTypes: Choose two types of creatures that you are specialized in hunting from the following list: Aberrations, Beasts, Celestials, Constructs, Dragons, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, Giants, Monstrosities, Oozes, Plants, or Undead.
details.bonuses: You add your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} to Intelligence and Wisdom checks you make that relate to the creature types in your grimoire. For example, if Undead are in your grimoire, you add your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} to an Intelligence ({@skill History}) check to recall lore about an Undead creature you're investigating or a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check to identify claw marks left on a corpse by an Undead creature. If you are already proficient in a skill relating to the monster types in your grimoire, you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} instead.
details.languages: When you gain this feature, you also learn one language based on the chart below.

classFeatures.name: Weapon Mastery
classFeatures.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of two kinds of weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Longswords. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices. When you reach certain Monster Hunter levels, you gain the ability to use the {@variantrule mastery properties|XPHB} of more kinds of weapons, as shown in the Weapon Mastery column of the Monster Hunter Features table.

classFeatures.name: Fighting Style
classFeatures.description: You gain a {@feat Fighting Style|XPHB} feat of your choice.

classFeatures.name: Studied Response
classFeatures.description: You have learned the most effective way to slay a monster is to strike its most vulnerable area at the right moment. Too early, and your attack glances away. Too late, and the monster may eviscerate you. When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you targets you or another creature with a melee or ranged attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} before the attack roll to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against that creature. If the attack misses, you regain the use of your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Monster Hunter Subclass
classFeatures.description: You gain a Monster Hunter subclass of your choice. The Carver Guild, Devourer Guild, Occultist Guild, and Trapper Guild subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Monster Hunter levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Monster Hunter level or lower.

classFeatures.name: Ability Score Improvement
classFeatures.description: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Monster Hunter levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeatures.name: Expert Strike
classFeatures.description: You learn the weaknesses of monsters so that you know exactly where to strike to inflict the most damage. You can add your Intelligence modifier to the attack and damage rolls of your weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}.

classFeatures.name: Improved Monster Grimoire
classFeatures.description: You have expanded your Monster Grimoire with your experience and knowledge. You can select a third creature type to add to your Monster Grimoire from the following list: Aberrations, Beasts, Celestials, Constructs, Dragons, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, Giants, Monstrosities, Oozes, Plants, or Undead. You can't choose a creature type you have chosen before. All the benefits you gain from your Monster Grimoire now also apply to this new type. Additionally, your attack rolls with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} against creature types in your Monster Grimoire can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

classFeatures.name: Subclass Feature
